div1.entryFree:
Score, subst. 1) a notch made on a tally: our forefathers had no other books than the s. and the tally, H6A IV, 7, 38.
2) an account kept by notches, and hence account or reckoning generally: I am not fourteen pence on the s. Shr. Ind. 2, 25. he's an infinitive thing upon my s. H4B II, 1, 26. eat and drink on my s. H6B IV, 2, 80. he never pays the s. All's IV, 3, 253. strikes some --s away from the great compt, V, 3, 56. he parted well and paid his s. Mcb. V, 8, 52. strike off this s. of absence, Oth. III, 4, 179.
3) (cf. Threescore, Fourscore etc.) a number of twenty: Rom. II, 4, 145. Lr. I, 4, 140. Cymb. III, 2, 70. eight s. Oth. III, 4, 174. Oth. III, 4, 174 as easy as a cannon will shoot point-blank twelve s. (viz yards) Wiv. III, 2, 34. his death will be a march of twelve s. H4A II, 4, 598. a would have clapped i' the clout at twelve s. H4B III, 2, 52. Followed by of: Tp. V, 174. H4B III, 2, 55. H4B III, 2, 55 R3 I, 2, 257. Tim. III, 6, 87. Cymb. III, 2, 69. Of omitted: Shr. I, 2, 111. II, 360. H4A II, 4, 5.
